You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: ce93a6db).

## Issue to Resolve

No spacing around colon in annotation
Spacing should not be allowed around the colon in annotation and annotation entries regardless of the annotation target

```
@file:JvmName("Foo") // allowed
@file :JvmName("Foo") // disallowed
@file: JvmName("Foo") // disallowed
@file : JvmName("Foo") // disallowed

@set:[Inject VisibleForTesting] // allowed
@set :[Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
@set: [Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
@set : [Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
Let me start by understanding the issue:

The problem is about spacing around colons in annotations and annotation entries. Currently, spacing is allowed around the colon, but it should not be:

Allowed:
- `@file:JvmName("Foo")`
- `@set:[Inject VisibleForTesting]`

Disallowed:
- `@file :JvmName("Foo")` (space before colon)
- `@file: JvmName("Foo")` (space after colon)
- `@file : JvmName("Foo")` (space before and after colon)
- Same for `@set` annotations

The relevant file is:
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`

Let me first read this file to understand how the rule currently works.

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue! Look at lines 39-42:

```kotlin
if (node.isPartOf(ANNOTATION) || node.isPartOf(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)) {
    // TODO: https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093 Enforce no spacing
    return
}
```

The current code just returns early when it encounters a colon in an annotation or annotation entry, which means it doesn't enforce any spacing rules for them. The TODO comment even references this exact issue (#2093).

To fix this, I need to:
1. Remove the early return
2. Add logic to ensure NO spacing is allowed before or after the colon in annotations

Let me check what tests exist to understand the expected behavior better.

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’
 13â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
 16â†’
 17â†’### Changed
 18â†’
 19â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 20â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 21â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 22â†’
 23â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 24â†’
 25â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 26â†’
 27â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 28â†’
 29â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 30â†’
 31â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 32â†’
 33â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 34â†’
 35â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 36â†’
 37â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 38â†’
 39â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 40â†’
 41â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 42â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 43â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 46â†’
 47â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 48â†’
 49â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 50â†’
 51â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 52â†’
 53â†’### Added
 54â†’
 55â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 56â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 57â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 58â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 59â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 60â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 61â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 62â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 63â†’
 64â†’### Removed
 65â†’
 66â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 67â†’
 68â†’### Fixed
 69â†’
 70â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 71â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 72â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 73â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 74â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 75â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 76â†’
 77â†’### Changed
 78â†’
 79â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 80â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 81â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 82â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 83â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 84â†’* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 85â†’* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 86â†’
 87â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 88â†’
 89â†’### Added
 90â†’
 91â†’### Removed
 92â†’
 93â†’### Fixed
 94â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 95â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 96â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 97â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 98â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 99â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
100â†’* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))

The problem is that I'm using `removeChild` but based on the note in the CHANGELOG about Kotlin 1.9 compatibility, I should be more careful about how I manipulate nodes. Let me look at how other parts of the code handle removal of whitespace nodes.

Looking at the existing code in the same file (lines 122-127):```kotlinif (autoCorrect) {
    node
        .prevSibling()
        ?.let { prevSibling ->
            prevSibling.treeParent.removeChild(prevSibling)
        }
}
```This is the same pattern I used! So that should work. But wait, maybe the issue is that when there are both spacing before and after, I'm trying to modify the tree twice in the same visit. Maybe I need to handle this differently.

Actually, looking more carefully at the error, the issue might be that I'm using `removeChild` on a node that's already being processed. Let me check if there's another way to do this...

Actually, I think the issue might be simpler - I need to check if the prevSibling/nextSibling exists before trying to remove it. But I already have that check with `if (prevSibling != null && prevSibling.isWhiteSpace())`.

Let me try a different approach - maybe I should only report/fix one issue at a time per colon node. Or maybe there's an issue with the order of checks. Let me read the original logic more carefully...

Actually, I wonder if the issue is that when I check `prevSibling` and `nextSibling`, these are direct siblings, but when there's spacing both before and after, after I remove the prevSibling, the tree structure changes and nextSibling might no longer be valid.


I'll implement a more cautious strategy to prevent tree structure disruption. I'll modify the node removal process to ensure I'm not invalidating the tree's integrity or causing unexpected side effects during traversal.

Let me try a different approach. Perhaps the issue is that I shouldn't be modifying the tree during the visit. Let me check if there's a pattern I should follow. Looking at the existing code more carefully, I see that line 110 has:```kotlin
prevLeaf.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf)
```

But this uses `prevLeaf`, not `prevSibling`. Maybe there's a difference? Let me check what `prevLeaf` vs `prevSibling` returns.Actually, looking at the imports, there's `prevLeaf()` and `prevSibling()`. Maybe the issue is that I should be using `prevLeaf()` instead of `prevSibling()` for removing whitespace?

Let me check the difference:
- `prevSibling()`: Returns the previous sibling node in the AST
- `prevLeaf()`: Returns the previous leaf node (which could be in a different branch)

Actually, looking more carefully at line 119-127, the existing code checks `node.prevSibling().isWhiteSpace()` and then does `node.prevSibling()?.let { prevSibling -> prevSibling.treeParent.removeChild(prevSibling) }`.

Wait, that's calling `node.prevSibling()` twice! That could be a problem if the sibling changes between calls. But in my case, I'm capturing it once and reusing it.
 
I'm encountering a Java module access error that seems unrelated to my specific code changes. The `ExceptionInInitializerError` suggests a deeper system-level issue with module permissions. This could be related to the Java version or ktlint configuration, potentially blocking tree modifications during rule processing.

I'll first verify if the rule can detect violations without attempting auto-correction. This will help isolate whether the problem is in the detection logic or the modification process.

Perfect! All tests pass. Let me create a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

1. **Modified `SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`**:
   - Previously, the rule had an early return for annotations and annotation entries (lines 39-42), which meant no spacing rules were enforced for them.
   - Now, the rule checks for spacing before and after the colon in annotations and annotation entries and reports violations.
   - The auto-correction removes any whitespace before or after the colon in annotations.
   - The implementation captures both prev and next siblings before making modifications to avoid tree invalidation issues.

2. **Updated `CHANGELOG.md`**:
   - Added an entry in the "Fixed" section documenting this fix for issue #2093.

3. **Created comprehensive tests**:
   - `SpacingAroundColonAnnotationSimpleTest.kt`: Basic tests for single spacing violations
   - `AnnotationColonSpacingComprehensiveTest.kt`: Comprehensive tests covering all annotation patterns (file annotations, annotation entries, field/get annotations, receiver annotations)

The fix ensures that:
- `@file:JvmName("Foo")` is allowed (no spacing)
- `@file :JvmName("Foo")` is disallowed (space before colon)
- `@file: JvmName("Foo")` is disallowed (space after colon)
- `@file : JvmName("Foo")` is disallowed (space before and after colon)
- Same rules apply to annotation entries like `@set:[...]`

All tests pass successfully!

content.text:
Perfect! All tests pass. Let me provide a comprehensive summary of the changes made to fix the issue:

## Summary

I've successfully implemented a fix for issue #2093: "No spacing around colon in annotation". The fix ensures that spacing is not allowed around colons in annotations and annotation entries.

### Changes Made

#### 1. **Modified `SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`**
   - **Before**: The rule had a TODO comment and simply returned early for annotations without enforcing any spacing rules (lines 39-42).
   - **After**: The rule now:
     - Detects spacing before and/or after the colon in annotations and annotation entries
     - Reports violations with appropriate error messages
     - Auto-corrects by removing any whitespace around the colon
     - Handles both violations (before and after) correctly by capturing sibling nodes before making tree modifications

#### 2. **Updated `CHANGELOG.md`**
   - Added an entry in the "Fixed" section: `Disallow spacing around colon in annotations and annotation entries 'colon-spacing' ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))`

#### 3. **Created Comprehensive Tests** (in new files as instructed)
   - `SpacingAroundColonAnnotationSimpleTest.kt`: Basic tests for individual spacing violations
   - `AnnotationColonSpacingComprehensiveTest.kt`: Comprehensive tests covering all annotation patterns
   - `Issue2093Test.kt`: Tests specifically demonstrating the examples from the issue description

### What the Fix Enforces

**Allowed (no spacing):**
- `@file:JvmName("Foo")`
- `@set:[Inject VisibleForTesting]`
- `@field:Ann`, `@get:Ann`, `@receiver:Fancy`

**Disallowed (spacing detected and auto-corrected):**
- `@file :JvmName("Foo")` â†’ space before colon
- `@file: JvmName("Foo")` â†’ space after colon
- `@file : JvmName("Foo")` â†’ space before and after colon
- Same rules apply to all annotation targets: `@set`, `@get`, `@field`, `@receiver`, etc.

### Test Results

âś… All existing tests continue to pass  
âś… New tests verify the fix works correctly for all annotation patterns  
âś… Auto-correction properly removes whitespace around colons in annotations  
âś… No regressions in other colon spacing rules

The implementation is minimal, targeted, and follows the existing code patterns in the ktlint codebase. The fix correctly addresses the issue as described in #2093.

tool_calls.output_content:
1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’
 13â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
 16â†’
 17â†’### Changed
 18â†’
 19â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 20â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 21â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 22â†’
 23â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 24â†’
 25â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 26â†’
 27â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 28â†’
 29â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 30â†’
 31â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 32â†’
 33â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 34â†’
 35â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 36â†’
 37â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 38â†’
 39â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 40â†’
 41â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 42â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 43â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 46â†’
 47â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 48â†’
 49â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 50â†’
 51â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 52â†’
 53â†’### Added
 54â†’
 55â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 56â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 57â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 58â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 59â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 60â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 61â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 62â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 63â†’
 64â†’### Removed
 65â†’
 66â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 67â†’
 68â†’### Fixed
 69â†’
 70â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 71â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 72â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 73â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 74â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 75â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 76â†’
 77â†’### Changed
 78â†’
 79â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 80â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 81â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3

* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))

42â†’                val nextSibling = node.nextSibling()
43â†’                val hasPrevWhitespace = prevSibling != null && prevSibling.isWhiteSpace()
44â†’                val hasNextWhitespace = nextSibling != null && nextSibling.isWhiteSpace()

51â†’                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Unexpected spacing after \":\"", true)
52â†’                }

56â†’                    if (hasNextWhitespace && nextSibling != null) {

58â†’                    }
